# BillGuard

> A second opinion on your medical bill. Check its charges against official CMS Medicare reference rates and draft a dispute letter.

BillGuard is an anonymous, informational web tool. A user uploads a medical or dental
bill (image or PDF); it reads the billing codes and charges with OCR, compares each code
to two official CMS fee schedules (the Physician Fee Schedule and the Clinical Laboratory
Fee Schedule), flags charges that are well above the Medicare reference rate, and can
draft a dispute letter. There are no accounts. Uploads are processed in memory and never
stored. Codes not found in the CMS data are marked "Unverified" and never counted as
overcharges.

## Pages
- [Home](https://billguard.kyuruki.cc/): what BillGuard is and how to start.
- [How it works](https://billguard.kyuruki.cc/how-it-works): the OCR → CMS-comparison pipeline, step by step.
- [FAQ](https://billguard.kyuruki.cc/faq): how overcharges are flagged, what CMS rates mean, privacy, and affiliations.
- [Privacy](https://billguard.kyuruki.cc/privacy): how a bill is handled (in memory, not stored).
- [About](https://billguard.kyuruki.cc/about): why the tool exists and its independence.
- [Analyze a bill](https://billguard.kyuruki.cc/analyze): the tool itself.

## Important context
- Informational only; not legal, medical, or financial advice.
- Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to CMS, Medicare, or any insurer.
- Medicare reference rates are a benchmark, not a statement of what a patient owes; a
  charge above the reference rate can be legitimate and is a prompt to request an
  itemized justification, not an accusation.
